creator: Julie Bort
title: (No AI) Anthropic’s Claude popularity with paying consumers is skyrocketing
dc:creator: Julie Bort
content: Estimates for total Claude consumer users are all over the map (we've seen figures ranging from 18 million to 30 million). Anthropic hasn't disclosed this data, but a spokesperson did tell TechCrunch that Claude paid subscriptions have more than doubled this year.
contentSnippet: Estimates for total Claude consumer users are all over the map (we've seen figures ranging from 18 million to 30 million). Anthropic hasn't disclosed this data, but a spokesperson did tell TechCrunch that Claude paid subscriptions have more than doubled this year.

title: (No AI) TikTok’s policy for AI ads isn’t working
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![A screenshot taken from a Samsung video promoting AI editing tools.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/Samsung-AI-video-promotion.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Samsung, like many companies using generative AI in their advertising, hasn’t placed an AI label on several videos shared through its TikTok accounts, and the fine print doesn’t always contain the answers. | Image by Samsung

I've been struggling to tell whether the ads appearing in my TikTok feeds have been made with generative AI tools. As someone who spends a great deal of time scrutinizing images and videos for the usual "tells" that something was synthetically generated, some of the promotions I've seen have definitely sparked suspicion. For several weeks, I didn't see any examples with the AI disclosure required by TikTok's advertising policies, however, so I had no way of knowing for sure.

What irks me is that _someone_ knows for sure if the content is AI-generated. They're just not telling the rest of us. And if companies that [claim to support AI-labelling …](https://www.theverge.com/tech/885727/samsung-execs-unpacked-ai-deepfake-photos-vs-reality-c2pa)

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/900400/tiktok-ai-ads-labels-samsung-disclosure)

Samsung, like many companies using generative AI in their advertising, hasn’t placed an AI label on several videos shared through its TikTok accounts, and the fine print doesn’t always contain the answers. | Image by Samsung

For several weeks, I didn't see any examples with the AI disclosure required by TikTok's advertising policies, however, so I had no way of knowing for sure.
What irks me is that someone knows for sure if the content is AI-generated. They're just not telling the rest of us. And if companies that claim to support AI-labelling …

title: (No AI) Why OpenAI killed Sora
author: Hayden Field
content:
![Stylized image of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, seated](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/09/STK201_SAM_ALTMAN_CVIRGINIA_B.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

On Tuesday morning, everything was business as usual at OpenAI. By the end of the day, [the company had announced](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/899850/openai-sora-ai-chatgpt) that it would scrap its video-generation app, Sora, and reverse plans for video generation inside ChatGPT; [it would wind down](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/899850/openai-sora-ai-chatgpt) a $1 billion Disney deal; it would shuffle the role of a high-level executive; and it would raise an additional $10 billion from investors, adding up to more than $120 billion total for its latest funding round.

OpenAI is now in a frenzy to turn a profit, or at least lose less money. Since its launch, Sora seems to have taken up a massive amount of compute without the financial return to justify it. Indus …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/902368/openai-sora-dead-ai-video-generation-competition)

OpenAI is now in a frenzy to turn a profit, or at least lose less money. Since its launch, Sora seems to have taken up a massive amount of compute without the financial return to justify it. Indus …

title: (No AI) AI Research Is Getting Harder to Separate From Geopolitics

content: A policy change announced by NeurIPS, the world’s leading AI research conference, drew widespread backlash from Chinese researchers this week and then was quickly reversed.
